adjudication_notes: Official Ontario exemplar labeled Level 4. Teacher summary: The student uses two or more related ideas in the same sentence (more than once). The style used is very expressive.

reasoning: uses two related thoughts in several sentences to develop ideas
the description is enhanced by many supporting details
communication: the description is clear and interesting
the writer clearly communicates his feelings (i.e., still his favourite toy even after his brother broke it)
uses different patterns of simple, complete sentences
teacher_notes.comments: The student uses two or more related ideas in the same sentence (more than once). The style used is very expressive.

teacher_notes_text:
Reasoning
- uses two related thoughts in several sentences to develop ideas
- consistently links ideas to topic
- the description is enhanced by many supporting details
Communication
- the description is clear and interesting
- the writer clearly communicates his feelings (i.e., still his favourite toy even after his brother broke it)
- uses different patterns of simple, complete sentences
Organization
- the writing flows naturally
Conventions
- includes accurate spelling
- uses capitals and periods
- writing is legible and neat
Comments
The student uses two or more related ideas in the same sentence (more than once). The style used is very expressive.

adjudication_notes: Official Ontario exemplar labeled Level 3. Teacher summary: This piece contains mostly simple ideas but includes a fair amount of supporting detail.

reasoning: develops some ideas using the conjunctions “but” and “when”
includes many ideas that are connected to the topic
provides supporting details and a description that makes sense
communication: describes the toy clearly, indicating its importance by words such as “put it in a special spot” and “carefully”
uses both simple and complete sentences
organization: groups common ideas to tell a story
includes complex sentences that use the conjunctions “but” and “when”
conventions: generally uses capitals and periods accurately
uses phonetic spelling for unfamiliar words (“ceap” for “keep” or “kept”,“cafoly” for “carefully”, “puling” for “pulling”, “trie” for “try”)
teacher_notes.comments: This piece contains mostly simple ideas but includes a fair amount of supporting detail.

teacher_notes_text:
Reasoning
- develops some ideas using the conjunctions “but” and “when”
- includes many ideas that are connected to the topic
- provides supporting details and a description that makes sense
Communication
- describes the toy clearly, indicating its importance by words such as “put it in a special spot” and “carefully”
- uses both simple and complete sentences
Organization
- groups common ideas to tell a story
- includes complex sentences that use the conjunctions “but” and “when”
Conventions
- generally uses capitals and periods accurately
- uses phonetic spelling for unfamiliar words (“ceap” for “keep” or “kept”,“cafoly” for “carefully”, “puling” for “pulling”, “trie” for “try”)
Comments
This piece contains mostly simple ideas but includes a fair amount of supporting detail.

adjudication_notes: Official Ontario exemplar labeled Level 2. Teacher summary: The description uses simple ideas. There is some confusion whether the toy is good or bad. Some errors in using capitals and periods.

reasoning: includes only simple ideas connected to the topic
communication: uses simple sentences with most using the same sentence stem (“My toy is ...”)
organization: there is some evidence of grouping in the list (“good” and “bad”)
conventions: makes some errors in the use of capitals and periods
uses both phonetic and conventional spelling
teacher_notes.comments: The description uses simple ideas. There is some confusion whether the toy is good or bad. Some errors in using capitals and periods.

teacher_notes_text:
Reasoning
- includes only simple ideas connected to the topic
- provides some supporting details
Communication
- uses simple sentences with most using the same sentence stem (“My toy is ...”)
- the description is usually clear
- some contradictory feelings are evident
Organization
- there is some evidence of grouping in the list (“good” and “bad”)
Conventions
- makes some errors in the use of capitals and periods
- uses both phonetic and conventional spelling
Comments
The description uses simple ideas. There is some confusion whether the toy is good or bad. Some errors in using capitals and periods.

adjudication_notes: Official Ontario exemplar labeled Level 1. Teacher summary: Although the student uses complete sentences and some phonetic and conventional spelling, only one idea is presented.

reasoning: includes only one simple idea (where the child plays with the toy)
communication: uses a few simple sentences that rely mainly on a single sentence stem (“I like to ...”)
conventions: includes both phonetic and conventional spelling
teacher_notes.comments: Although the student uses complete sentences and some phonetic and conventional spelling, only one idea is presented.

teacher_notes_text:
Reasoning
- includes only one simple idea (where the child plays with the toy)
- provides no supporting details
Communication
- includes little description
- uses a few simple sentences that rely mainly on a single sentence stem (“I like to ...”)
Organization
- the writing resembles a list
Conventions
- uses capitals randomly
- has some spacing problems
- includes both phonetic and conventional spelling
Comments
Although the student uses complete sentences and some phonetic and conventional spelling, only one idea is presented.
